name: Llama Guard
source_version: distribution source recorded; canonical governance package not established
allowed_use_boundary: Use as safety-classification model distribution crosswalk evidence.
Preserve the limitation that the recorded source is a distribution page, not a complete canonical model-governance record.
claims: Llama Guard is presented as a safety-classification model family for classifying model inputs and outputs against a safety taxonomy.
non_claims: Llama Guard output does not establish execution authority.
The recorded distribution page does not establish complete model provenance or governance coverage.
A classifier result does not prove transition admissibility or certification.

transition_table_mapping.source_version: distribution source recorded; canonical governance package not established
transition_table_mapping.claims: classification of model inputs and outputs against a safety taxonomy
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no complete provenance, policy authority, standing, certification, or execution authority
transition_table_mapping.input_artifact_type: model input, output, taxonomy, prompt template, threshold, and runtime configuration
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: model distribution and runtime identity; authority not established
transition_table_mapping.evidence_model: distribution source plus local bounded crosswalk
transition_table_mapping.policy_or_rule_model: taxonomy and classifier behavior dependent on version and configuration
transition_table_mapping.decision_or_result_model: safety category or classifier result only
transition_table_mapping.receipt_or_trace_model: captured classifier inputs, outputs, configuration, and local compatibility record
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: exact model, prompt, taxonomy, threshold, runtime, and output required
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: classifier evidence may inform safety review
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: missing exact model version, prompt template, taxonomy, threshold, runtime, raw output, or authority overclaim

fail_closed_conditions: canonical model or distribution version is missing
taxonomy, prompt, threshold, or runtime is absent
classifier behavior is generalized without fixtures
comparative behavior is claimed without executable evidence
